{"id":"project-LICHEN-w4vx","title":"SECURITY: No rate limiting on want_config requests","description":"File: lichen/subsys/lichen/meshtastic/adapter.c:1788-1790

  case LICHEN_MESHTASTIC_TO_RADIO_WANT_CONFIG_ID:
      adapter->stats.want_config_count++;
      return dispatch_want_config(adapter, msg.value.want_config_id);

A malicious client can spam want_config requests, causing the adapter to repeatedly:
1. Snapshot the nodedb (CPU intensive with sorting)
2. Encode multiple protobuf messages
3. Enqueue many messages to the output queue

This is a denial-of-service vector. The want_config_count stat tracks this, but no limiting is enforced.

Consider:
1. Rate limiting want_config to once per N seconds
2. Rejecting want_config if one is already in progress
3. Documenting this as a known limitation for callers to handle

SECURITY: Resource exhaustion attack via config request flood.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:49:58.647123Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:49:58.647152Z"}
